headline: 84.3% of clips have AI-identifiable brands — 15.700000000000003% have none
insight: 161 of 191 highlight clips have at least one brand detected by AI. Note: brand detection has only 20% reproducibility — the same clip may detect different brands on re-analysis.

pricing.reasoning: StatsBomb charges $50K-200K/yr for sports analytics subscriptions. For a niche sport at early stage, brand intelligence could be $500-$2,000/month per brand subscriber. At 10 brand subscribers: $5K-$20K/month total.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Brand detection agreement is only 20%. The '84.3%' figure may not be stable — a re-run could produce a different number. Needs prompt engineering to improve reliability.

headline: Top detected brands: JOOLA(405), Nike(51), HEAD(5)
insight: Brand frequency across 191 clips: {'JOOLA': 405, 'Nike': 51, 'HEAD': 5, 'On': 5, 'Franklin': 4}. CRITICAL CAVEAT: 97% of clips have no identified venue. If most clips come from Lifetime facilities (a JOOLA partner), JOOLA dominance is a venue artifact, not a market signal.

pricing.reasoning: ShotTracker = $15K/court/yr. Brand tracking at venue level: $200-500/venue/month.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: If clips are from a Lifetime venue, JOOLA is the house paddle brand. Dominance reflects distribution deals, not organic preference. Multi-venue data required to separate signal from noise.

headline: Sponsorship whitespace: Gatorade(125), BODYARMOR(120), Nike(87) — zero detected presence
insight: METHODOLOGY: The AI prompt asks Gemini to identify common sports brand categories. These categories (['Gatorade', 'BODYARMOR', 'Nike', 'Yeti', 'Selkirk']) were flagged as ABSENT — meaning zero instances detected across 191 clips. This does NOT mean these brands aren't in pickleball — it means they weren't visible in these specific highlight clips from this venue.
evidence: Method: AI-flagged absence, not confirmed market gap

pricing.reasoning: Novel data product with no direct comparable. Conservative estimate: $300-$1,000/month per brand subscriber for whitespace alerts across venues.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Absence of brand detection ≠ absence of brand. The AI may miss logos, and these clips are from ~1 venue. A brand like Gatorade could be at 500 other pickleball venues and not appear in this sample.

headline: Paddle detection: JOOLA dominates (124/191 clips) — but likely a venue artifact
insight: Paddle brands detected: {'JOOLA': 124, 'CRBN': 2, 'Selkirk': 1, 'HEAD': 1, 'Franklin': 1}. JOOLA appears in 124 clips. Lifetime facilities use JOOLA as their house paddle, so this dominance almost certainly reflects the venue's equipment deal, not organic player preference. Multi-venue data needed.

pricing.reasoning: Paddle market intelligence: $500-$2,000/month for equipment manufacturers. At 5 paddle brand subscribers: $2.5K-$10K/month total.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: JOOLA provides house paddles at Lifetime venues. This is not organic market share — it's distribution. Remove Lifetime clips and JOOLA dominance likely disappears.
